title: [Handshake] Inconsistent type for "indexing" values
body:
Currently, `ControlMergeOp`'s second result (which contains the index of the selected input) is required to be an `Index` type (see [here](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/main/include/circt/Dialect/Handshake/HandshakeOps.td#L413)). This seems slightly inconsistent with the `selectOperand` of `MuxOp` which can either be an `Index` type or an `IntegerType` (as specified by its [verification function](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/main/lib/Dialect/Handshake/HandshakeOps.cpp#L415)) and carries the same semantic meaning (i.e., indexing into a list of operands).

Additionally, once we have a bitwidth optimization pass, the required `Index` type will prevent us from optimizing the second result of `ControlMergeOp`'s, since we won't be able to change it to e.g., an `i1` if the control merge only has two inputs.

It seems to me that we should implement the same sort of logic for this result as for the `selectOperand` of `MuxOp`, for the reasons outlined above. This means having the second result of `ControlMergeOp` be an `AnyType` and checking that the value is either an `Index` or `IntegerType` with a large enough width in the operation's `verify` method. 

This is something I can address quickly if people think this is a good idea.
